Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a past XV Años dance project.

imageUrls.description: URLs of images associated with the project.

videoUrls.description: URLs of videos associated with the project.

PortfolioItem.title: PortfolioItem
PortfolioItem.description: Represents a single item in the portfolio, like a video or image of a choreography.

id.description: Unique identifier for the portfolio item.

title.description: Title of the portfolio item (e.g., 'Vals de Ensueño').

description.description: A short description of the item.

category.description: Category or genre of the dance (e.g., 'Salsa', 'Bachata', 'Vals').

mediaUrl.description: URL of the video or image file.

thumbnailUrl.description: URL of a thumbnail image, especially for videos.

PriceList.title: PriceList
PriceList.description: Represents pricing information for different services.

id.description: Unique identifier for the price list entry.

currency.description: Currency in which the price is specified.

PyrotechnicsMachine.title: PyrotechnicsMachine
PyrotechnicsMachine.description: Represents information about a pyrotechnics machine.

id.description: Unique identifier for the pyrotechnics machine.

description.description: Description of the pyrotechnics machine and its capabilities.

imageUrl.description: URL of an image of the pyrotechnics machine.

definition.description: Collection of past XV Años dance projects. Publicly readable.
Collection of portfolio items (videos and images). Publicly readable.

params.name: itemId
params.description: Unique identifier for the portfolio item.

definition.description: Collection of pricing information for different services. Publicly readable.

params.name: priceListId
params.description: Unique identifier for the price list entry.

definition.description: Collection of information about pyrotechnics machines. Publicly readable.

params.description: Unique identifier for the pyrotechnics machine.

firestore.reasoning:
Given the requirement for a catalog-style application where users primarily browse content without authentication requirements beyond anonymous access, the Firestore structure is designed for ease of access and scalability.  Since no user-generated content is present, and the application is read-heavy, we optimize for simple queries and minimal security rule complexity.

Authorization Independence: Not strictly applicable in this scenario, as all data is public. No denormalization is required.

QAPs: The structure supports secure 'list' operations by segregating data into dedicated collections, each representing a specific content type (projects, portfolio items, price lists, pyrotechnics machines).  This segregation allows for straightforward security rules that permit unauthenticated read access to these collections.

The design intentionally avoids any user-specific data storage or complex authorization logic.  All collections are treated as public read-only catalogs.

Future considerations: if any kind of admin panel is required, a separate admin-only collection to define admins by existence can be used to restrict write access to those collections.
